text.title: Jira Project Setup, Issue Types, and Workflow Design
text.summary: Rules for project type selection, issue-type hierarchy, workflow state machine design, and board configuration in Jira.

list.item: Scrum: sprints, backlogs, velocity charts — for teams with predictable feature work in timeboxes.
Kanban: continuous flow, WIP limits, cumulative flow diagram — for support, ops, or platform teams.
Bug Tracking: simplified workflow — for pure defect-tracking projects.
Jira Service Management: ITIL-flavored request portal — for customer-facing support queues.

rule.statement: Limit workflows to 5-7 statuses maximum; use one shared workflow per issue type across projects rather than bespoke per-project workflows.
rule.rationale: Per-project workflow proliferation creates scheme sprawl, breaks cross-project JQL queries, and slows the entire Jira instance through scheme-lookup overhead.

example.code:
statuses: - name: "To Do" category: "To Do" - name: "In Progress" category: "In Progress" - name: "Code Review" category: "In Progress" - name: "QA Testing" category: "In Progress" - name: "Done" category: "Done"
transitions: - from: "To Do" to: "In Progress" trigger: "Start Work" - from: "In Progress" to: "Code Review" trigger: "Submit for Review" condition: "Branch linked" - from: "Code Review" to: "QA Testing" trigger: "Approve" - from: "QA Testing" to: "Done" trigger: "Pass QA"

rule.statement: Place required field validators on transitions, not on issue creation; this reduces friction for bulk imports and agent-driven flows without sacrificing data quality at the stage where it matters.
rule.rationale: Creation-time required fields block bulk migrations and bot-driven flows unless service accounts are explicitly exempted.

antipattern.description: Adding global transitions to "Done" from any state.
antipattern.reason: Global-to-Done transitions let issues skip QA and review stages, producing false "Done" counts and audit trail gaps.

list.item: Epic: large feature grouping with Start/End Date for roadmap visibility.
Story: user-facing functionality with Story Points and Acceptance Criteria.
Task: technical work items with Estimate and Component fields.
Bug: defects with Severity and Steps to Reproduce; can exist at any hierarchy level.
Sub-task: breakdown of parent issue, linked to parent.

rule.statement: Resist creating new issue types for every workflow nuance; add a custom field or label instead.
rule.rationale: New issue types require scheme updates, affect all projects using the shared scheme, and multiply configuration complexity without proportional value.

rule.statement: Limit custom fields aggressively; share them via Global context rather than project-scoped context; Jira has a 200-field issue-view cap and performance degrades well before it.
rule.rationale: Custom field sprawl is the most common cause of Jira performance degradation in large instances. Each field adds a database column that is loaded even when not displayed.

example.code: custom_fields: - name: "Story Points" type: number screen: "Edit Issue" context: "Global" - name: "Acceptance Criteria" type: text_area screen: "Create/Edit Issue" context: "Global" - name: "Code Reviewer" type: user_picker screen: "Edit Issue" context: "Global"
